@graph.name: Unicode to Krutidev Converter
@graph.description: Convert Devanagari Unicode (Mangal/Arial Unicode MS) text to Krutidev 010 font instantly in your browser. 100% private, no upload required.

acceptedAnswer.text: Unicode Devanagari is the modern digital text standard. Characters are encoded semantically, making them readable natively across all computers, smartphones, social media, and search engines without any custom font files.

Krutidev is a legacy Remington typewriter layout font. It encodes Hindi characters visually onto standard English ASCII keyboard keycodes, meaning the Krutidev font must be installed locally to read the text.

mainEntity.name: Why does converted text look like English characters?

acceptedAnswer.text: Because Krutidev is a visual font mapping, the converted text is written in English keystroke characters. When you apply the Krutidev 010 font family to this text, those English characters will display as Devanagari glyphs.

mainEntity.name: How do I view Krutidev output correctly?

acceptedAnswer.text: Copy the converted output and paste it into Microsoft Word or any publishing program. Select the pasted text, and change the font family name to Krutidev 010 or Kriti Dev 010.
Yes. The conversion is processed 100% locally in your browser. Your text is never uploaded to any server, ensuring complete confidentiality of your documents.

mainEntity.name: Does this work for Marathi text as well as Hindi?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Since Marathi is also written in Devanagari script, Unicode Marathi text converts to Krutidev 010 the same way Hindi text does. Just paste your Unicode text as usual.

mainEntity.name: Can I upload a Word or PDF file instead of pasting text?

acceptedAnswer.text: Currently this tool works with pasted or typed text only. Copy the Unicode text from your Word, Excel, or PDF file, paste it here, convert it, then paste the Krutidev output back into your document. Direct file upload may be added in a future update.
